engineGuide.h2: Mitsubishi Ek Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Mitsubishi Ek across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 0.7L Petrol
entries.history: Primary kei engine for Ek range; SOHC 12-valve design, 48-54hp output across variants.
compatibleVariants: eK Wagon H81W, eK Wagon H82W, eK Classic, eK Active, eK Space, eK Custom
commonFailures: Timing belt failure at 60k-80k miles if not replaced preventatively
Oil consumption increase due to piston ring wear on high-mileage units
Carbon buildup on intake valves causing rough idle and power loss

entries.title: 0.7L Petrol

commonFailures: Timing belt tensioner wear leading to belt jump
EGR valve clogging on urban-use vehicles

ECU software issues affecting idle stability

commonProblems.h2: Mitsubishi Ek Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Mitsubishi Ek's 3G83 kei engine range is generally robust but high-revving use and grey import mileage can trigger specific failures. Timing belt wear, oil consumption, and turbo issues on Sport variants dominate replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Timing Belt Failure - Why Mitsubishi Ek 3G83 Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Mitsubishi Ek eK Wagon H81W/H82W, eK Sport, eK X (2001-2019, 3G83, 3G83SOHC, 3G83T)
problems.rootCause: Timing belt tensioner wear and rubber degradation on high-rev kei engines, especially with irregular servicing.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Belt Kit Replacement - Before failure occurs
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing belt, tensioner, idler pulleys and water pump
repairOptions.longevity: 60k+ miles if maintained; only viable before catastrophic failure

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Belt Failure Damage Repair - After jump but before seizure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Cylinder head inspection, valve replacement, belt kit, labour
repairOptions.longevity: Variable; depends on valve/piston damage extent

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned Engine - Post-seizure or major damage
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 3G83 unit with upgraded timing components, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Long-term solution; addresses root cause with specialist rebuild

problems.recommendation: On a Mitsubishi Ek worth £1,500-£4,000, preventative timing belt replacement at 60k miles is far more economical than post-failure repair. If the engine has already suffered belt jump, get quotes for a rebuilt unit - the cost difference versus head repair is often minimal, and you gain warranty protection.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 3G83 engine prices for your Mitsubishi Ek

problems.h4: Oil Consumption - Why High-Mileage Mitsubishi Ek Engines Burn Oil
problems.affectedModels: Mitsubishi Ek eK Wagon, eK X, eK Active (2005-2019, 3G83, 3G83-659)
problems.rootCause: Piston ring wear and valve stem seal hardening on high-rev kei engines, exacerbated by short-trip urban driving.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Top-End Refresh - Address valve seals and PCV
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace valve stem seals, PCV valve, gaskets; decarbonise intake
repairOptions.longevity: May reduce consumption temporarily; not a permanent fix for ring wear

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Piston Ring Replacement - Major engine work
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Engine removal, piston ring replacement, honing, reassembly
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if block is sound; labour-intensive on kei engines

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned Engine - Comprehensive solution
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 3G83 with upgraded rings and seals, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage engines; eliminates uncertainty

problems.recommendation: If your Ek is consuming over 1L per 1,000 miles and has exceeded 80k miles, a rebuilt engine often represents better value than ring replacement. The labour cost to access pistons on a kei engine is high, and a specialist rebuild includes warranty protection.
problems.cta: Get quotes for oil-consumption fixed Mitsubishi Ek engines

problems.h4: Turbo Failure - Why Mitsubishi Ek Sport 3G83T Engines Need Specialist Attention
problems.affectedModels: Mitsubishi Ek eK Sport H81W/H82W, eK ROAR (2002-2013, 3G83T, 3G83T-IC)
problems.rootCause: Turbocharger oil seal wear and bearing failure due to high-rev kei driving patterns and inadequate oil change intervals on grey imports.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Turbo Rebuild Kit - If caught early with no engine damage
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove turbo, replace seals/bearings, balance, reinstall
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if no oil contamination entered engine; requires expert diagnosis

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Turbo Replacement + Engine Flush - Post-failure contamination
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New/rebuilt turbo, engine oil flush, filter changes, labour
repairOptions.longevity: Risk of residual damage; monitor oil consumption post-repair

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned Engine with Turbo - Comprehensive
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 3G83T unit with new turbo, upgraded seals, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Safest option; addresses both turbo and potential engine damage

problems.recommendation: Turbo failure on a 3G83T often contaminates the engine with metal particles. Unless caught extremely early, we recommend a full rebuilt engine with new turbo to avoid repeat failures. The cost difference versus turbo-only repair is often marginal once labour is factored.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 3G83T turbo engine prices for your Mitsubishi Ek Sport

problems.h4: Carbon Buildup - Why Mitsubishi Ek 3G83 Engines Lose Power Over Time
problems.affectedModels: Mitsubishi Ek eK X B11W/B33W/B36W, eK Active (2013-2019, 3G83UPD, 3G83EMI)
problems.rootCause: Direct injection variants and EGR recirculation cause carbon deposits on intake valves and ports, restricting airflow and causing rough idle.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Intake Decarbonisation Service - Preventative or early symptoms
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Walnut blasting or chemical cleaning of intake ports and valves
repairOptions.longevity: Effective for mild buildup; recommended every 30k miles on direct injection variants

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - EGR Delete + Cleaning - Recurring buildup mitigation
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: EGR valve blanking, intake cleaning, ECU remap to prevent codes
repairOptions.longevity: Reduces future buildup; check UK MOT/emissions compliance before proceeding

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned Engine - Severe damage or neglected maintenance
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 3G83 with cleaned/updated intake system, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Only necessary if carbon has caused valve damage or severe compression loss

problems.recommendation: Carbon buildup is preventable with regular decarbonisation services every 30k miles. If your Ek shows rough idle and power loss but compression is good, start with professional cleaning. Only consider engine replacement if valves are damaged or compression is low.
problems.cta: Get quotes for decarbonised Mitsubishi Ek 3G83 engines

problems.h4: Cooling System Failure - Why Mitsubishi Ek Engines Overheat
problems.affectedModels: Mitsubishi Ek eK X B36W 4WD, eK Space, eK Wagon (2001-2019, all 3G83 variants)
problems.rootCause: Radiator hose deterioration, water pump seal failure, and thermostat sticking on ageing kei engines, especially with infrequent coolant changes.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Cooling System Refresh - Preventative maintenance
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace radiator hoses, thermostat, water pump, flush and refill coolant
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended every 5 years or 60k miles; prevents overheating damage

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Overhead Damage Repair - Post-overheat head gasket
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Cylinder head removal, gasket replacement, machining, reassembly
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if block is undamaged; compression test essential before proceeding

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned Engine - Severe overheating damage
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 3G83 with updated cooling components, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best option if block is warped or pistons are damaged from overheating

problems.recommendation: Preventative cooling system maintenance is critical on high-mileage grey import Ek models. If overheating has occurred, get a compression test before committing to head gasket repair - often a rebuilt engine offers better long-term value with warranty protection.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt Mitsubishi Ek engine prices with cooling system upgrades

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mitsubishi Ek
finalCta.paragraph: The Mitsubishi Ek's compact 3G83 kei engine is reliable when maintained, but grey import mileage and high-rev driving can trigger specific failures. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Mitsubishi Ek specialists who understand JDM chassis compatibility. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare prices and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mitsubishi Ek Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mitsubishi Ek and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

faq.h2: Mitsubishi Ek Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Mitsubishi Ek engine replacement cost UK grey import?
items.answer: Mitsubishi Ek 3G83 engine replacement typically costs £850-£2,800 for a rebuilt unit supply-only, depending on variant and turbo specification. Supply & fit adds £450-£950 depending on garage location and chassis complexity.
keyPoints: Rebuilt 3G83 SOHC engines: £850-£2,100 supply only
Turbo 3G83T variants: £1,400-£3,200 due to specialist rebuild requirements
Supply & fit service adds £450-£950 labour depending on location
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Mitsubishi Ek engine prices from UK specialists

items.question: What engine codes fit my Mitsubishi Ek chassis?
items.answer: The Mitsubishi Ek exclusively uses 3G83 family engines (657cc/659cc petrol). Compatibility depends on chassis code: H81W/H82W use early 3G83, while B11W/B33W/B36W use updated 3G83UPD variants.
keyPoints: H81W/H82W chassis: 3G83, 3G83SOHC, 3G83T (2001-2013)
B11W/B33W/B36W chassis: 3G83UPD, 3G83EMI, 3G83LC (2013-2019)
Always verify chassis code before ordering to ensure mounting compatibility
items.cta: Use our free 3G83 engine code checker for instant Ek compatibility

items.question: How long does Mitsubishi Ek engine replacement take UK?
items.answer: Typical turnaround is 7-14 working days from quote to delivery for rebuilt Mitsubishi Ek engines, depending on variant availability and specialist workload. Supply & fit appointments add 1-2 days for installation.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engine sourcing: 3-7 working days average
Delivery to UK mainland: 1-3 working days via specialist courier
Specialist fitting appointment: 1-2 days depending on garage schedule
items.cta: Get your Mitsubishi Ek engine replacement timeline estimate today

items.question: What warranty comes with Mitsubishi Ek replacement engines UK?
items.answer: Every rebuilt Mitsubishi Ek engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK specialists. Used engines typically carry 3-6 month warranties depending on supplier.
keyPoints: Rebuilt/reconditioned units: 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard
Used engines: 3-6 month warranty typical, verify terms before purchase
Warranty covers parts and labour for manufacturing defects, not misuse
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Ek engine warranty terms from verified UK suppliers

items.question: Can I fit a different engine size in my Mitsubishi Ek kei car?
items.answer: Engine swaps in the Mitsubishi Ek are possible but may affect kei car classification, insurance and MOT compliance. Sticking with the original 3G83 family ensures regulatory compliance and easier specialist support.
keyPoints: Kei car regulations limit engines to 660cc; larger swaps void classification
Insurance companies may decline cover for non-standard engine installations
Specialist rebuilds of original 3G83 offer best balance of performance and compliance
items.cta: Speak to a Mitsubishi Ek specialist about compliant engine options

items.question: How do I know if my Mitsubishi Ek needs engine replacement?
items.answer: Key signs your Mitsubishi Ek 3G83 needs replacement include excessive oil consumption (over 1L/1,000 miles), knocking noises, loss of compression, or catastrophic timing belt failure. Get a specialist diagnostic to confirm.
keyPoints: Oil consumption over 1L per 1,000 miles indicates ring or seal wear
Metallic knocking or rattling suggests bearing or timing component failure
Compression test below 120psi on any cylinder warrants replacement consideration
items.cta: Get a specialist Mitsubishi Ek engine diagnostic consultation today

items.question: Are Mitsubishi Ek 3G83 engines reliable after rebuild?
items.answer: Yes, Mitsubishi Ek 3G83 engines are reliable after specialist rebuild when upgraded components address original failure points like timing tensioners and piston rings. Choose a rebuilder with kei car expertise.
keyPoints: Specialist rebuilds include upgraded timing components and piston rings
Proper break-in procedure and oil change intervals critical for longevity
12-month warranty provides peace of mind on rebuilt unit quality
items.cta: Compare rebuilt 3G83 engine quotes from kei car specialist rebuilders

items.question: What is the difference between 657cc and 659cc 3G83 engines?
items.answer: The 659cc 3G83-659 is an updated variant introduced post-2010 for stricter kei emissions compliance, producing marginally more power (52-54hp vs 48-52hp) with revised ECU mapping.
keyPoints: 659cc variant offers slightly higher output and better emissions compliance
Mounting points generally compatible but ECU calibration differs
Verify chassis code and ECU compatibility before ordering replacement
items.cta: Check Mitsubishi Ek 657cc vs 659cc compatibility for your chassis

items.question: Can I get a Mitsubishi Ek engine supply and fit service UK?
items.answer: Yes, EngineMarket's network includes UK garages experienced with Mitsubishi Ek kei car chassis offering supply & fit packages. Prices typically add £450-£950 to engine cost depending on location and variant complexity.
keyPoints: Network garages familiar with H81W, B33W, B36W mounting specifications
Service includes ECU matching, immobiliser programming and road test
Single-point warranty covering both engine and fitment labour available
items.cta: Book Mitsubishi Ek engine supply & fit with a JDM specialist garage

items.question: How do I choose between used, rebuilt and reconditioned Mitsubishi Ek engines?
items.answer: Choose used Mitsubishi Ek engines for budget repairs with verified low mileage; rebuilt for best long-term value with warranty; reconditioned for balanced cost and reliability when core engine is sound.
keyPoints: Used: £650-£1,600, budget option, verify compression before purchase
Rebuilt: £850-£2,800, upgraded components, 12-month warranty, best value
Reconditioned: £800-£2,300, cleaned and resealed, suitable for moderate wear
items.cta: Compare all Mitsubishi Ek engine type options and prices in one place
